quiz.question:
Black has reached the queenside pawns. 

Does White have a way to win?

answers.text: Yes, White can win creating a passed pawn.

feedback.text: a6 wins. Notice how the c6-pawn prevents the black king from stopping the pawns easily. The king would have to take a longer route and then the e-pawn comes into play!

distractors.text: No. The pawns on the queenside will be lost and White cannot do anything.

feedback.text: Are you sure? Here's a hint: calculate a6.

distractors.text: It should be a draw because Black will take the queenside pawns and White will take the h- and g-pawns.

feedback.text: It's impossible to win both kingside pawns because the g-pawn is a protected passer. The king cannot take these pawns because when the h-pawn is taken, the g-pawn runs.
